"Steward" redirects here. For others with the same name, see Steward (disambiguation).
Order of stewards
Stewards are responsible for an assortment of critical functions (from Game of Thrones Blu-ray)
Type Order
Region Gift, Wall
Allegiance Night's Watch
Current Leader Bowen Marsh
Founded Age of Heroes

The order of stewards is one of the three orders that comprise the Night's Watch, the others being the builders and the rangers. The stewards' main duty is to supply the Watch with food, clothing, and anything else needed for war.[1] A senior member, known as the Lord Steward or First Steward, leads the order.[2]

Tasks[ ]

The stewards are responsible for an assortment of critical functions, providing vital day-to-day services for their brothers on the wall. They hunt and farm, tend horses, gather firewood, cook meals, make clothing, maintain weapons, and bring the supplies needed by the Night's Watch from the south.[3][4] Personal stewards can be assigned to higher officers (e.g. Jeor Mormont[2] and Ser Mallador Locke)[5][6] for whom they might also squire,[7] or to a maester (e.g. Aemon.[3])

Despite the fact that making war upon the wildlings is not among the tasks of the stewards, they are trained in arms, as the first duty of all the brothers of the Night's Watch is to defend the Wall.[1]

Recent Events[ ]

A Storm of Swords[ ]

Samwell Tarly tending to a raven, by Zezhou Chen © Fantasy Flight Games

With Jeor Mormont, Lord Commander of the Night's Watch, leading two hundred brothers north of the Wall during the great ranging, most of the four hundred men who remain at Castle Black are builders and stewards.[8]

Several members of the Night's Watch, including multiple stewards, plot to kill the lord commander at the Fist of the First Men, but their plans are interrupted by the fight at the Fist.[9] Later, several of the stewards participate in the mutiny at Craster's Keep.[10]

A Feast for Crows[ ]

Clubfoot Karl, Orphan Oss and Muttering Bill, former stewards since their desertion, remain at Craster's Keep following the mutiny, along with several other of their brothers.[6]

A Dance with Dragons[ ]

Bowen Marsh, the Lord Steward, betrays Lord Commander Jon Snow in the mutiny at Castle Black.[11]

Known Stewards[ ]

Current Stewards[ ]

  • Bowen Marsh, Lord Steward or First Steward
  • Arron[12]
  • Bass[4]
  • Borcas[2]
  • {Chett}, at first a personal steward of Maester Aemon,[3] later the dog handler[2][13]
  • {Clubfoot Karl},[5] who deserts the Night's Watch after taking part in the mutiny at Craster's Keep.[10][6]
  • Clydas, personal steward of Maester Aemon.[3]
  • Cuger, in the appendices mistakenly listed as Cugen[13][5][6][14]
  • Dannel[14]
  • {Dareon},[2] who eventually deserts the Night's Watch while in Braavos,[15] after having been assigned the position of wandering crow[16]
  • {Donal Noye}, the blacksmith of Castle Black.
  • Donnel Hill, also known as Sweet Donnel, steward to Ser Mallador Locke[5][6]
  • {Easy}[5]
  • Eddison Tollett, personal steward to Lord Commander Jon Snow[6]
  • {Hake}, a cook[5]
  • Hobb, the chief cook[5]
  • Jeren[5][6]
  • Jon Snow, personal steward to Lord Commander Jeor Mormont[2]
  • Left Hand Lew[5][6]
  • {Maslyn}[5]
  • Mully[5][6]
  • {Muttering Bill},[5] who deserts the Night's Watch after taking part in the mutiny at Craster's Keep.[10][6]
  • {Old Henly}[5]
  • {Orphan Oss},[5] who deserts the Night's Watch after taking part in the mutiny at Craster's Keep.[10][6]
  • Owen the Oaf[6]
  • {Red Alyn of the Rosewood}[5]
  • {Rolley of Sisterton},[5] part of the mutiny at Craster's Keep, where he dies[10]
  • Samwell Tarly, one of the personal stewards of Maester Aemon[2]
  • Satin, Lord Commander Jon Snow's personal steward and squire[17]
  • Sawwood[5]
  • {Small Paul}[5]
  • Tim Tangletongue[5][6]
  • Ty[14]
  • Wallace Massey, steward to Denys Mallister, the commander at Eastwatch-by-the-Sea[5][6][14]
  • Wick Wittlestick[6]

Historical stewards[ ]

  • Rat Cook, a character from a legend of the Night's Watch.

Quotes[ ]

You think it's easy work, fit for cowards? The order of stewards keeps the Watch alive. We hunt and farm, tend the horses, milk the cows, gather firewood, cook the meals. Who do you think makes your clothing? Who brings up supplies from the south? The stewards.[3]

—Chett to Jon Snow
